title: SatForge
description: A from-scratch CDCL SAT solver studio that grew an SMT brain. The SAT core does two-watched literals, VSIDS, first-UIP learning and restarts, emits machine-checkable DRAT proofs of UNSAT and re-verifies them in-browser with an independent RUP/RAT checker, counts models exactly (#SAT, component caching), extracts minimal unsat cores (MUS), and optimizes — a complete weighted MaxSAT engine (linear SAT-UNSAT and core-guided WPM1 over a Generalized Totalizer) plus encoders for N-Queens, Sudoku, coloring, Hamiltonian cycles, integer factoring (a multiplier circuit run backwards) and the zebra puzzle. NEW: a full DPLL(T) SMT solver built on the very same engine — SAT lifted to first-order theories. A lazy DPLL(T) loop abstracts a formula's Boolean skeleton, and on each candidate hands the theory atoms to from-scratch decision procedures: EUF (equality + uninterpreted functions) by proof-producing congruence closure with curried applications and minimal conflict explanations; linear real & integer arithmetic (QF_LRA/QF_LIA) by a general simplex (Dutertre–de Moura) over EXACT δ-rationals — no floating point anywhere — with conflict explanations and integer branch-and-bound; and mixed QF_UFLIA by Ackermann reduction so uninterpreted functions and arithmetic combine soundly without Nelson-Oppen. Ships a tolerant SMT-LIB 2 parser and an SMT Studio with a curated example library (congruence closure, strict ordering cycles, integer pigeonhole, UF+arithmetic) that shows the verdict, the model, and the exact theory lemmas the solver learned to close the search. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, and 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an independent Ackermann+FM reference — part of 185 self-tests in all.
